items.scenario: You are a sophomore enrolled in a literature seminar this semester. Your professor has just posted a list of three possible final research topics on the course website, and asked each student to write back with a preference and a brief rationale before next Monday.
items.direction: Write an email to Professor Hale. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which of the three topics interests you most
Explain why this topic connects to your previous coursework
Ask whether you may combine elements from two of the topics
items.subject: Final research topic preference for the seminar

items.scenario: You recently started a summer internship at a small marketing firm. Your manager has invited you to share early impressions of the onboarding process during a check-in next week. You have noticed both helpful practices and areas that confused you during your first two weeks.
items.direction: Write an email to Ms. Brennan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe one part of the onboarding you found especially helpful
Explain a step in the process that left you unclear about expectations
Suggest a small change that could help future interns
items.subject: Early thoughts ahead of our onboarding check-in

items.scenario: You volunteer at a neighborhood youth center that runs a free weekend tutoring program. The coordinator recently sent a survey asking long-term volunteers for ideas about a new outdoor learning session planned for the spring. Several parents have already shared informal feedback with you.
items.direction: Write an email to Kevin. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the kind of activity parents seem most interested in
Suggest a location near the center that could work well
Offer to help recruit additional volunteers for the session
items.subject: Ideas for the spring outdoor learning session

items.scenario: You recently bought a wireless reading lamp from an online store. The lamp stopped charging after only one week, and the seller has asked customers to report any defects directly by email rather than through the website form. You still have the original receipt and packaging at home.
items.direction: Write an email to the Returns Team. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem you noticed with the lamp
Provide the details of when and how you purchased it
Request information about the next steps for a replacement
items.subject: Defective reading lamp purchased last week
